element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Foundation for understanding unit types and attack ranges
Understanding how roads, woods, mountains affect movement and defense
Learning positioning and indirect vs. direct attack trade-offs
Managing armies across multiple units with different roles
Strategic layer enabling unit bonuses and special powers
Resource management via cities, ports, airports, bases
Managing conflicts against 2-4 opposing armies simultaneously
Strategic constraint requiring unit retreat or resupply logistics

typical_relief_triggers: Successful capture of enemy HQ or routing all enemy units
Securing resource buildings (cities, airports) to fund new units
Reaching designated survival day count on defensive maps
Eliminating a threatening enemy unit with superior firepower
typical_tension_triggers: Introduction of new enemy faction into ongoing conflict
Enemy unit with superior range approaching player position
Low ammunition on primary weapons forcing secondary weapon use
Multiple enemies converging on single player unit
Loss of key resource-generating property to enemy capture
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Turn-based tension cycle: players experience decision pressure during their turn (unit positioning, attack ordering, resource allocation), then relief/reactive tension during enemy turns where outcomes resolve. Longer maps create extended tension arcs over multiple days. Campaign missions introduce narrative escalation alongside mechanical complexity.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: War Room mode contains optional challenge maps beyond campaign narrative path; multiplayer maps offer alternative engagement

difficulty_curve.early_game: Tutorial emphasis through Field Training mode with simplified mechanics. Campaign begins with 1v1 conflicts teaching basic unit roles and terrain advantages. Slow introduction of unit variety.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Introduction of three-faction conflicts and commanding officer abilities. Maps expand in size and complexity. Resource management becomes critical as ammo depletion forces tactical retreats. Multiple simultaneous threats require advanced positioning.
difficulty_curve.late_game: Four-way conflicts and narrative climax. Complex map layouts with mixed terrain forcing routing decisions. High-level commanding officers with powerful abilities. Maps with alternative victory conditions (capture X cities vs. route all enemies) increase strategic depth.
notable_spikes: Transition from 1v1 to 3-faction warfare (plot escalation point)
Introduction of advanced CO abilities shifting tactical balance
Maps with time limits (survive N days) creating decision pressure acceleration

notable_level_patterns.description: Most maps allow two victory conditions: eliminate all enemy units or capture opponent's headquarters. Creates strategic fork allowing aggressive (routing) or efficient (HQ rush) playstyles.
Maps emphasizing city/port/airport capture as primary resource flow. Controlling more properties generates more income for unit production, creating economy-vs-firepower tension.
Missions with survival objectives requiring player to hold positions for N days while under siege. Rewards conservation and terrain mastery over aggression.
3-4 faction maps where player must manage threats from multiple directions simultaneously. Terrain becomes critical for funneling enemies or creating defensive chokepoints.
Extended maps where unit ammunition depletion forces strategic withdrawal, resupply phases, or secondary weapon fallback. Creates rhythm of aggression -> supply → aggression cycles.

sources_used: wikipedia - Advance Wars (used for turn-based mechanics, terrain system, unit interactions, battle system framework, and campaign structure applicable to Wars series)
